_**User**_

Before going to phase 5; I would like to now if it's a good idea to update the list of registrations page. 
Right now we only show if the Identity is completed or not for a case, but we have multiple other steps now. 

---

Exploring the registrations list and registration workflow so we can give informed advice.

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I now have enough context to advise on updating the registrations page.

The registrations list currently shows only the Identity status. The detail page already displays a completeness checklist with three chips: Identity, Legal residence, and Address. Household, housing situation, and civil status lack checklist flags because they are data collection steps rather than core decisions. Only the four core decisions defined by ADR-0004 map to checklist flags.

Phase 5 concerns duplicate detection, so updating the list page is a separate UX decision. Currently, only Identity appears on the list, while the detail page shows three checklist items. After phases 2 through 4, officers cannot see residence or address progress without opening each case. The key question is what to display versus what to omit.

Show only the domain checklist flags on the list page: Identity, Legal residence, and Address declared. Household, civil status, and housing situation should remain off the list because they lack checklist flags and are not core approval decisions. Phase 5 NR linking may appear as a detail banner rather than a list column. This change is safe to do now as a small polish before phase 5 begins.

Deferring the update until Phase 7 would be too late for daily officer workflow. Four UI patterns are under consideration: adding separate Residence and Address icon columns, a single compact progress column, a progress fraction or bar, or a work queue filter for incomplete checklist items.

The technical scope is small. Extend the RegistrationCaseSummary DTO with LegalResidenceEstablished and AddressDeclared. Map these fields in ListRegistrationCasesHandler. Update RegistrationCaseList.razor. No new domain logic is required.

Do not add household or civil status columns yet. Do not add NR or BIS status until Phase 5. AddressConfirmed and RegisterDeterminable are reserved for future phases.

Progress indicators on the list page align with the work queue design system. They help triage cases that need attention.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — updating the list page is a good idea, and it fits better as a small polish **before** Phase 5 than as part of Phase 5 itself.

## The gap today

The list and detail pages are out of sync.

**List** (`RegistrationCaseList.razor`) only exposes identity:

```61:83:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseList.razor
                    <MudTh Class="app-table-header">Identity</MudTh>
                </HeaderContent>
                <RowTemplate Context="item">
                    ...
                    <MudTd DataLabel="Identity">
                        @if (item.IdentityEstablished)
                        {
                            <MudIcon Icon="@Icons.Material.Outlined.CheckCircle" ... />
                        }
```

**Detail** already shows three checklist items:

```45:53:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor
        <AppSection Title="Completeness checklist">
            <MudStack Row="true" Spacing="2" Wrap="Wrap.Wrap">
                <AppStatusChip Label="@($"Identity {(_case.Checklist.IdentityEstablished ? "✓" : "—")}")" ... />
                <AppStatusChip Label="@($"Legal residence {(_case.Checklist.LegalResidenceEstablished ? "✓" : "—")}")" ... />
                <AppStatusChip Label="@($"Address {(_case.Checklist.AddressDeclared ? "✓" : "—")}")" ... />
```

The handler only maps one flag:

```5:24:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/ListRegistrationCases/ListRegistrationCasesHandler.cs
public sealed record RegistrationCaseSummary(
    ...
    bool IdentityEstablished);
...
                c.Checklist.IdentityEstablished))
```

After Phases 2–4, an officer scanning the queue cannot tell whether a case still needs residence or address work without opening every case.

## What to show (and what not to)

Your architecture deliberately separates **checklist flags** from **intake steps**. Per [ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-checklist-over-linear-state-machine.md), the meaningful completeness signals are domain flags, not every UI section.

| Step on detail page | Has checklist flag? | Show on list? |
|---------------------|---------------------|---------------|
| Identity | `IdentityEstablished` | Yes |
| Legal residence | `LegalResidenceEstablished` | Yes |
| Address | `AddressDeclared` | Yes |
| Housing situation | No | No (for now) |
| Household | No | No |
| Civil status | No | No |
| Documents | No dedicated flag | No (optional later) |

Household, civil status, and housing are real work, but they are not part of the checklist model yet. Adding columns for them would either duplicate detail-page logic or invent UI-only booleans, which goes against ADR-0004.

Future flags like `AddressConfirmed` (Phase 6 police) and `RegisterDeterminable` (Phase 7 approval) belong on the list when those phases land — not now.

## Why before Phase 5, not during it

Phase 5 is **National Register search & BIS** — duplicate detection when linking/creating a person. That is mostly a detail-page concern (search dialog, duplicate banner), not a list-triage concern.

Updating checklist progress on the list:

- Is independent of NR search
- Uses data you already persist on `RegistrationCase.Checklist`
- Is a small, low-risk change (extend `RegistrationCaseSummary`, map two more fields, update the table)
- Improves daily officer workflow right now

So: do it as a quick alignment pass before Phase 5; do not fold it into Phase 5 scope.

## Recommended UI approach

Avoid three separate icon columns if the table already feels wide. Better options:

1. **Single “Progress” column** — three compact checkmarks or chips inline (same visual language as the detail checklist). Best balance of scanability and width.
2. **Summary fraction** — e.g. `2/3` with a tooltip listing Identity / Residence / Address. Very scannable for queues.
3. **Filters** — “Missing residence”, “Missing address” in `AppFilterPanel` (design system list template already anticipates filters). Complements columns; does not replace them.

I would not add person name to the list yet unless you also add it to the summary DTO and search — NR search in Phase 5 may make that more valuable later.

## What I would defer

- NR/BIS link status on the list → Phase 5
- Police verification status → Phase 6
- “Ready for review” / `RegisterDeterminable` → Phase 7 (`GetCaseReviewChecklist`)
- Household / civil status columns → only if the domain gains explicit completeness rules

## Bottom line

**Yes, update the list** — but scope it to the **three checklist flags** the detail page already shows, not every intake step. That closes the real UX gap without fighting your domain model, and it is worth doing now rather than waiting for Phase 5 or Phase 7.

If you want this implemented, switch to Agent mode and I can wire up `LegalResidenceEstablished` and `AddressDeclared` on the summary DTO and refresh the table UI.

---

_**User**_

ok do it as you said

---

Proceeding with the list page update.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
No MudTooltip exists in the project. I'll use aria-labels on icons, following the existing identity column pattern. Short text labels or title attributes may provide native tooltips.

A small reusable component would be cleaner than inline markup. I'll check the Registration/Components folder for existing display component patterns.

I decided to create a dedicated `RegistrationCaseProgress.razor` component after all. It accepts the three boolean flags and uses a `MudStack` row with an aria-label showing the completed count. This keeps the list page clean and avoids repeating the icon block.

The component defines three boolean parameters for the checklist flags and a computed property to count completed items. A small `ChecklistItem` sub-component may be inlined within the same file.

The component will be named `RegistrationCaseChecklistProgress.razor` to match domain naming. Documentation updates will be skipped unless tests fail.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Updating the handler DTO and list UI, and adding a small reusable progress component.


_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

Done. The registration case list now shows checklist progress aligned with the detail page.

### Changes

1. **`RegistrationCaseSummary`** — extended with `LegalResidenceEstablished` and `AddressDeclared`, mapped from the domain checklist in `ListRegistrationCasesHandler`.

2. **`RegistrationCaseChecklistProgress.razor`** — new component showing `2/3` plus three icons (identity, legal residence, address), with accessible labels.

3. **`RegistrationCaseList.razor`** — replaced the single **Identity** column with a **Progress** column using the new component.

The list API response now includes all three checklist flags for HTTP clients as well. Build and all 78 tests pass. Refresh `/registration/cases` in the running app to see it.
